reasoning: The Item 19 pro forma statements are based on 11,332 independent franchisee traditional restaurants whose royalties during the covered period were 4% of Gross Sales. However, current new franchisees opening after January 1, 2024 pay a 5% royalty. This 100 basis point difference means the pro forma Operating Income Before Occupancy Costs overstates the economics for new franchisees by approximately 1% of Gross Sales (e.g., $40,020 per year at average sales of $4,002,000). The pro forma OIBOC of $734,000 at $3M sales would be approximately $704,000 after adjusting for the higher royalty, and the $879,000 at $3.4M would be approximately $845,000.
quote_fragments: The royalties payable by these franchised restaurants during the covered period were 4% of Gross Sales
The royalty of 5% of Gross Sales applies on and after January 1, 2024
